title: Gymnopteris tricuspis
taxonomy: Gymnopteris tricuspis belongs to the family Osmundaceae, which is a group of ancient and primitive ferns. This species is classified as a member of the genus Gymnopteris, a genus of extinct and extant ferns. The classification of Gymnopteris tricuspis is also supported by its morphological characteristics, which are similar to other members of the Osmundaceae family. The species is further classified as a member of the phylum Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that produce spores. Gymnopteris tricuspis is also classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Gymnopteris tricuspis, also known as the Three-pointed Rupture Fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Gymnopteris tricuspis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnopteris tricuspis is typically prepared as a decoction or a paste, and is also used in the form of infusions and timctures.
